ann401_contradiction_resolution.rule_ANN401: Flags use of 'Any' in type annotations (dynamically typed return/parameter). Does NOT flag bare 'list' or bare 'tuple' unless they are typed as 'Any'.
ann401_contradiction_resolution.project_config: ANN401 is in the global ruff ignore list (pyproject.toml line 76: ignore = ['ANN101','ANN102','ANN401',...]). The rule fires for zero files in this project.
ann401_contradiction_resolution.dr008_migration_error: Three # noqa: ANN401 markers were introduced during the DR-008 migration on the coerce_attributions validators. These were inert (the rule was already globally suppressed) and the description in the previous closure report was inaccurate ('on validator return types (tuple/list)'). ANN401 is about Any, not about tuple/list.
ann401_contradiction_resolution.resolution: All three newly-introduced dead # noqa: ANN401 markers removed in DR-008A source commit. Return type annotation tightened from bare 'tuple' to concrete 'tuple[object, ...]'. mypy: 0 issues.

remaining_suppressions.exact_signature: def non_empty(cls, v: list) -> list:  # noqa: ANN401
remaining_suppressions.why_necessary: Pydantic v2 field_validator receives the already-coerced field value as a plain list. The annotation 'list' here is a Pydantic validator pattern, not a typed public API. ANN401 does not fire for this rule (ANN401 = Any, not list). The # noqa: ANN401 is therefore inert. It predates DR-008 and is outside this migration's scope.
remaining_suppressions.classification: PRE-EXISTING — inert dead suppression (ANN401 globally ignored). Unrelated to DR-008 migration. Accurately classified; not hiding interface incompatibility.
